interactions.summary: Interviewer questions conscription expansion (age of reserve enlistment and draft revival); Jiang answers with resource/elite-control framing and links draft to AI governance control.
The interviewer acknowledges Jiang’s repeated worst-case stance while Jiang states he hopes to be wrong but insists preparation for negative scenarios is prudent.

speaker_notes.note: Interviewer references a Palantir headline and framing of automatic Selective Service registration, importing headline framing into the interview.

claims.claim: Jiang interprets proposals to raise reservist enlistment age and draft mechanisms as signs of a depopulation/elitist control trend.
He argues military draft expansion and food/information control can work together as elements of a future AI-driven obedience architecture.
He reiterates that social and mental preparedness matters even when predictions may be wrong, emphasizing resilience under a worst-case assumption.

signature_moments.source_phrase: get rid of most of us
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Condenses the source’s strongest accusatory logic about elites preferring coercive scarcity structures.

signature_moments.source_phrase: I hope I'm wrong... prepare for worst-case
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Captures both humility and persistent warning posture used to interpret risk.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A critique used by Jiang to describe policy choices that appear to reduce population pressure through structural coercion.
Automatic youth registration and draft pipeline potentially used by the state to scale wartime mobilization.

chronology_notes.note: Final policy section before close: moves from military and demographic control themes to emotional close on preparedness.

uncertainty_notes.note: Depopulation framing is rhetorical and not supported by external evidence in this source; keep confidence low and scoped to hypothesis level.
